Details: Based on two studies [2059, 2067], removal of 1,4-dioxane from water via chlorination was found to be ineffective (0 percent removal) at room temperature, with high doses of chlorine. One study [2059] was able to achieve 1,4-dioxane removal greater than 80 percent with pH 6, 75-degree Celsius water, and high doses of chlorine (1,000 mg/L). These conditions are not feasible for full-scale drinking water treatment.
